---
title: "Statistical Modeling 2"
subtitle: "Stretch modeling outside your comfort zone"
format:
  html:
    embed-resources: true
    toc: true
---

# Goal

Use an AI agent to attempt a model that is outside your current comfort zone, then document what you trust and what still needs review.

# Choose One Option

## Option A: Multilevel Negative Binomial Model

Dataset: `data/simulated/county_wellness_indicators_long.csv`

Task: Identify or create a count-like outcome and fit a model with county/state-style clustering if appropriate.

## Option B: Ordered Logistic Regression

Dataset: `data/simulated/neighborhood_health_survey_analysis.csv`

Task: Create an ordered outcome from blood pressure category or another ordinal variable and fit an ordered logit model.

## Option C: Latent Profile Model

Dataset: `data/simulated/neighborhood_health_survey_analysis.csv`

Task: Use continuous diet and blood pressure variables to identify profiles.

## Option D: Latent Class Model

Dataset: `data/simulated/neighborhood_health_survey_analysis.csv`

Task: Use categorical health, demographic, or behavior indicators to identify classes.

# Required Framing

Tell the AI tool you are new to the method and need it to explain the workflow, assumptions, diagnostics, and interpretation.

Treat the first answer as a draft. Make a serious attempt, then evaluate the output.

# Acceptance Criteria

Your report should include:

- Why you chose the model.
- What package or function was used.
- What assumptions or identification issues matter.
- Output and interpretation.
- At least two checks on whether the model is appropriate.
- A list of things that still need review.

# Work Area

```{r}
# Start here.
```
